title: Testing & Mocking
description: Python testing from pytest basics to Staff+ FAANG patterns: parametrize, fixtures, mocking, property-based testing, test doubles, coverage strategies, and CI integration.

sections.title: 1. pytest Fundamentals — Structure and Idioms
sections.description:
pytest is the industry standard. Tests are functions, not classes. Use assert directly — no self.assertEqual. Run with python -m pytest for consistent path resolution.

Key conventions: Files named test_*.py. Functions named test_*. Use pytest.raises() for expected exceptions, pytest.mark.parametrize for multiple cases, pytest.approx() for floats.

FAANG angle: 'How do you test a function that reads from a file?' — use pytest's tmp_path fixture. No mocking needed for filesystems.

sections.title: 2. Fixtures — Scoped Setup and Teardown
sections.description:
Fixtures are the backbone of pytest. Use scope to control reuse: function (default, every test), class, module, session (once per run).

Use yield for teardown — code after yield runs even if the test fails. Autouse fixtures apply to all tests without explicit injection.

FAANG angle: 'How do you share a database connection across tests?' — session-scoped fixture that creates a connection, yields it to all tests, and tears it down after the last test.

ponytail: Not covering conftest.py hierarchy here — it's the same pattern (just file-system-scoped).

sections.title: 3. Mocking — unittest.mock Patterns
sections.description:
Use unittest.mock.patch to replace external dependencies during tests. patch.object replaces a single attribute. side_effect controls return values per call. spec prevents attribute typos.

MagicMock has all dunder methods predefined. PropertyMock mocks @property. ANY matches any argument. call_args_list inspects all calls.

FAANG angle: 'Mock an API call that sometimes fails' — use side_effect with a list: [Response(200), Response(500), Response(200)].

sections.title: 4. Property-Based Testing
sections.description:
Instead of writing examples, state properties that must always hold. hypothesis finds edge cases you didn't think of.

Common properties: round-tripping (encode -> decode == original), idempotency (running twice == once), commutativity (a op b == b op a), and invariants (sorting never changes length).

FAANG angle: 'Test a sort function' — properties: (1) output is sorted, (2) output is a permutation of input (same elements). Hypothesis finds the bug in 2 seconds. Used extensively at Google for fuzzing.

sections.title: 5. Staff+ Patterns — Integration, Flaky Tests, and CI Strategy
sections.description:
Integration tests use real dependencies (databases, APIs) via testcontainers or Docker. Flaky test management: quarantine flaky tests (pytest.mark.flaky with reruns), triage by root cause (race condition, external dependency, timing).

Coverage strategy: Target 80%+ on new code. Use pytest-cov with branch coverage. Enforce coverage gates in CI. Exclude generated code and test files themselves.

Test pyramid at FAANG: 70% unit, 20% integration, 10% end-to-end. At Staff+ level, you design the testing strategy, choose the right balance, and write the shared test infrastructure.

FAANG Staff+ angle: 'Design a testing framework for a microservice architecture' — contract tests (Pact), consumer-driven contracts, canary testing in production, and a flaky test dashboard.
sections.codeBlock:
# pytest.ini content
[pytest]
filterwarnings = error
addopts = --strict-markers --tb=short --strict-config
markers =
    flaky: test that is intermittently flaky
    slow: test that takes > 5s
    integration: test requiring external services

# conftest.py for testcontainers integration
@pytest.fixture(scope='session')
def postgres_container():
    from testcontainers.postgres import PostgresContainer
    with PostgresContainer('postgres:15') as pg:
        yield pg

# Flaky test rerun example
@pytest.mark.flaky(reruns=3, reruns_delay=2)
def test_unreliable_service():
    import requests
    resp = requests.get('http://flaky-service:8080/health')
    assert resp.status_code == 200

sections.title: References & Further Reading
sections.description:
• pytest docs
  • Hypothesis — property-based testing
  • Testcontainers — integration testing with real services
  • Testing Python by David Sale
  • pytest Quick Start Guide by Bruno Oliveira

details:
FAANG testing workflow: (1) Write the test before the code (TDD for bug fixes, test-after for prototypes). (2) Use parametrize for edge cases. (3) Mock at the boundary (external I/O), not internally. (4) Add property-based tests for complex logic. (5) CI runs with pytest --random-order --timeout=60.

Common mistakes: Over-mocking (testing mocks, not behavior); mutable fixtures shared across tests (use yield + reset); not using pytest.approx for floats; testing implementation details instead of public API.

Staff+ expectations: Design the testing strategy for the team. Build shared fixtures and test infrastructure. Triage flaky tests and drive them to zero. Advocate for property-based tests in critical paths. Review test coverage metrics and enforce quality gates.
